message:
test(fcp-conformance/posture): pin PostureRequirement satisfaction + attestation validity (br-o8l2b)

PostureRequirement is the device-attestation gate every zone policy
uses to decide whether a device meets the posture floor. 7 variants
with documented satisfaction semantics including a deliberate
asymmetry between RequireTrue (fail-closed on missing) and
RequireFalse (fail-open on missing — absence treated as 'not
enabled'). Zero conformance pinning despite the policy-critical
nature.

Adds 22 tests against the public fcp_core posture API:

- RequireTrue: missing→fail, true→pass, false→fail
- RequireFalse: missing→PASS (documented fail-open),
  false→pass, true→fail
- RequireEqual: exact-match-required + missing→fail
- RequireOneOf: in-list passes, not-in-list fails, missing fails
- RequireMinVersion: semver-style comparison across 6 cases
- RequireMinValue: numeric >= across 5 cases
- RequireMaxValue: numeric <= across 4 cases
- type-mismatch returns false (no panic on String value where
  Number expected)
- attribute() returns the underlying key for all 7 variants
- PostureAttestation::SCHEMA == 'fcp.posture.v1' (verifier interop
  wire string)
- is_valid requires unexpired AND correct-schema (both gates)
- is_for_node compares NodeId
- get_attribute returns Some/None correctly
- PostureAttributeValue type-tag predicates (as_bool/as_str/
  as_number — only one matches for each variant)
- PostureAttributeKey::as_str snake_case (10 documented variants)
- Custom variant pass-through

All 22 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
posture_requirement_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
